message:
feat(rpc): include messages in agent_end, forward extension errors, fast-exit non-retryable

Three improvements to the RPC/stdin server mode (src/rpc.rs):

1. AGENT_END MESSAGE PAYLOAD
   The AgentEnd event handler now destructures the `messages` field and
   includes it in the serialized JSON sent to RPC clients:
     { "type": "agent_end", "messages": [...], "error": ... }
   Previously only the error field was forwarded, meaning RPC consumers
   had no way to retrieve the final conversation state from the event
   stream alone.

2. EXTENSION EVENT ERROR FORWARDING
   When ExtensionManager::dispatch_event() returns an error during
   agent event processing, the error is now forwarded to the RPC output
   channel as a structured event:
     { "type": "extension_error", "event": "<name>", "error": "<msg>" }
   Previously, extension dispatch failures were silently swallowed (the
   `let _ = ...` discarded the Result). This gives RPC clients visibility
   into extension-side failures that may affect agent behavior.

3. NON-RETRYABLE ERROR FAST EXIT
   In the retry loop within run_prompt_with_retry(), when a provider
   returns an Err (no HTTP response received), the error string is now
   checked against is_retryable_error(). If the error is NOT retryable
   (e.g., authentication failure, invalid model, bad request), the loop
   breaks immediately instead of consuming remaining retry attempts.
   This avoids wasting time and API quota on errors that will never
   succeed on retry. The error_hints are captured in both paths.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
